---
title: "An Introduction to Seven Flowers"
author: Felipe H.
date: 7/8/26
output: 
  html_document: 
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    highlight: zenburn
    theme: darkly
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(askpass)
library(knitr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



# Introduction

[Placeholder]

## Orange for reference

![](https://deltarune.wiki/images/Orange_overworld.png?cb=dk0kox&h=thumb.php&f=Orange_overworld.png){width="142" height="25"}

This is a short practice page based on seven flowers.

# General information: Table 1

```{r flowerDATA, echo=FALSE}
flowers <- c("Flowery", "Aqua", "Seth","Green","Orange","Yellow","Blue")
souls <- c("Determination","Patience","Perseverance","Kindness","Bravery","Justice","Integrity")
favNum <- c("7","23","41","56","2","71","89")
omegaFlowery <- data.frame(flowers, souls, favNum)
print(omegaFlowery)
```

# General information: Why are flowers important?

Flowers are important for a variety of reasons, including being an essential food source for a variety of animals by attracting pollinators, but also for human well being, as they provide distinctive colors and scents that we often enjoy and appreciate.

# Bar Chart 2

```{r value-chart}
read_excel("Holistic Data Set.xlsx")
df <- read_excel("Holistic Data Set.xlsx")
plot(df$Temp~df$pH)
```

# This isn't working fix on Tuesday
```{r base-bar-chart, echo=FALSE}
Temp <- c(30.6, 30.7, 26.8, 28, 28)
Movement <- c("Lo", "Len")
barplot(Temp,
        names.arg = Movement,
        col = "seagreen",
        main = "TheYeast",
        xlab = "Up",
        ylab = "Down")
```
